transformation_type: nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or aminoalcohols to larger-ring alcohol/ketone homologues
mechanism_type: diazonium-ion formation followed by Wagner-Meerwein-type 1,2-alkyl migration
common_conditions: generate diazonium precursor from aminomethylcycloalkane or aminoalcohol under nitrosating conditions, then rearrange to ring-expanded cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone products
description_short: Nitrosation-triggered ring-expansion chemistry of aminomethylcycloalkanes and aminoalcohols that furnishes cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through 1,2-shift rearrangement.

transformation_type: oxidation of alcohols or oximes to carbonyl compounds with hypervalent iodine(V) reagent
mechanism_type: alkoxyperiodinane formation followed by elimination to aldehydes/ketones
common_conditions: treat primary or secondary alcohol with Dess-Martin periodinane under mild neutral conditions to obtain aldehyde or ketone products
description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

transformation_type: intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters
mechanism_type: base-promoted intramolecular ester enolate acyl substitution / related Thorpe-Ziegler nitrile cyclization
common_conditions: deprotonate diester with strong base to form an enolate that cyclizes intramolecularly onto a second ester, then protonate the cyclic beta-keto ester product
description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

transformation_type: concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of diene and dienophile to cyclohexene derivatives
mechanism_type: pericyclic six-electron cycloaddition through an aromatic transition state
common_solvents: varied, often toluene|DCM|thermal or Lewis-acid conditions
common_conditions: combine a conjugated diene with an alkene or alkyne dienophile thermally or under catalysis to furnish cyclohexene or cyclohexadiene frameworks
description_short: Concerted [4+2] cycloaddition that rapidly generates six-membered rings with predictable stereo- and regioselectivity in normal or inverse electron-demand modes.

transformation_type: acid-, base-, or photochemically induced alkyl migration in cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols
mechanism_type: rearomatizing alkyl-shift rearrangement, commonly via sequential 1,2-shifts
common_conditions: expose ortho- or para-dienone substrate to rearrangement-promoting conditions to induce alkyl migration and rearomatization to a substituted phenol
description_short: Rearrangement of cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols driven by rearomatization, often proceeding through sequential 1,2-shifts and sensitive to substitution pattern and reaction conditions.
